_comment: TRUE POSITIVE — WZ-700006: AWS Root Account Login (Wazuh KQL)
scenario: AWS root account used for console login — root should never be used for routine operations
why_tp: Root account used — AWS best practice: never use root for routine operations
MFA not used for root login — critical security gap
Source IP is a Tor exit node (185.220.101.45)
Login at 03:00 UTC — off-hours for most regions
Confidence: 95 — root account logins are extremely rare and always warrant investigation
analyst_action: IMMEDIATE: Check if root account was used for any IAM/resource changes after login
Enable MFA on root account if not already enabled
Rotate root credentials and review root API keys (there should be none)
Verify if this was an authorized administrative action via out-of-band communication with account owner
Check AWS CloudTrail for all events from this session (typically short window of root use)
